body.title: GoogleSource paid model_id 'gemini-*-paid' not stripped before API call
body.description:
Pre-existing latent bug found 2026-06-19. The paid Google designer entry has model_id 'gemini-2.0-flash-paid' (registry) / routed via the 'google' source. GoogleSource._model_name only does removeprefix('google/') — it does NOT strip the '-paid' suffix, so a paid dispatch would call API model 'gemini-2.0-flash-paid' which is invalid (404). The '-paid' is a registry convention to distinguish the billing variant; the source must map it to the bare Google model name. NOT on the free path DS uses (google_free), so it didn't block the free-inference work — but the paid Gemini path is broken if ever selected. (Also bump the slug: 2.0-flash is retired → 2.5-flash, like the free entry.)

**Affected files:** devices/inference/sources.py (GoogleSource._model_name), devices/inference/models_registry.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN: strip '-paid' (and bump to a live slug) so the paid Gemini path resolves a valid model. OUT: deciding whether the paid path should be enabled at all (Akien prefers free).
**Completion criteria:** a designer dispatch routed to the paid 'google' source calls a valid Google model and completes.
**Test plan:** unit — _model_name('gemini-2.5-flash-paid') == 'gemini-2.5-flash'.
